fact_nuggets.text: Overgrow (Japanese: Fresh Greenery) is an Ability introduced in Generation III. All Grass-type first partner Pokemon have this Ability.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Boosts the power of Grass-type moves when HP is low. MD T D S Boosts the power of the Pokemon's Grass-type moves when its HP is low. B S L Conq. Raises Attack when HP is low. MD GtI When the Pokemon's HP is low, the power of its Grass-type moves goes up. * When the Pokemon's HP is low, the power of Grass-type moves goes up! * S MD When the Pokemon's HP is low, the power of Grass-type moves goes up! MD RT DX When the Pokemon's HP is low, the power of Grass-type moves goes up.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generations III to IV 1.1.2 Generations V onward 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generations III to IV [ edit | edit source ] When a Pokemon with Overgrow uses a Grass-type move , the move's power will be increased by 50% if the user has less than or equal to of its maximum HP remaining. Generations V onward [ edit | edit source ] Instead of boosting Grass-type moves' power, Overgrow now technically boosts the Pokemon's Attack or Special Attack by 50% during damage calculation if a Grass-type move is being used, resulting in effectively the same effect. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Overgrow has no effect outside of battle.

Generations V onward [ edit | edit source ] Instead of boosting Grass-type moves' power, Overgrow now technically boosts the Pokemon's Attack or Special Attack by 50% during damage calculation if a Grass-type move is being used, resulting in effectively the same effect.
Strengthens grass moves to inflict 1.5 damage at 1/3 max HP or less.
Boosts Grass-type moves at low HP
